title: A snake will gather its friends
display_title: A snake will gather its friends

chunks.content:
Article: A snake will gather its friends
[]: "A snake will gather its friends"             Quote Post by NAveryW » Sat Feb 21, 2009 12:02 am Yeah, what was that line supposed to mean?

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Sat Feb 21, 2009 12:03 am Everyone likes gathering their friends for get-togethers. Who doesn't like to party? Snakes have to get their grooves on, too.

[]:             Quote Post by EvaCub » Sat Feb 21, 2009 11:50 am it means that in times of trouble people gather together to overcome the obstacle it is also a phrase normally used in spite of the opposition

[]:             Quote Post by cat42 » Sat Feb 21, 2009 4:11 pm The Australian subtitles (which I'm convinced are the festival subtitles) translate the line as: "lt takes one old fox to know another, l guess."

[]:             Quote Post by Oedipusfoot » Sat Feb 21, 2009 4:33 pm Bad people congregate together?

[]:             Quote Post by Joseph the PRPD » Sun Feb 22, 2009 5:59 am Snakes usually represent evil and sneaky/cunning. So... Maybe someone who is evil or bad, possibly Keel Lorenz, will have his meet ups with the instrumentality team to devise a plan. But in Rebuild we already see all of the instrumentality -sound only- domino's chatting with Gendo.

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Sun Feb 22, 2009 4:02 pm cat42 wrote: The Australian subtitles (which I'm convinced are the festival subtitles) translate the line as: "lt takes one old fox to know another, l guess."

[]:             Quote Post by Sceleris » Sun Feb 22, 2009 8:49 pm 「国連軍はいいとしても、よく内務省と戦略自衛隊を説得できたものね」 「ま、いろいろと貸しがあるのよ」 「蛇の道は蛇ね……」 "Convincing the UN Forces is one thing, but the Home Ministry

Article: A snake will gather its friends
のね」 「ま、いろいろと貸しがあるのよ」 「蛇の道は蛇ね……」 "Convincing the UN Forces is one thing, but the Home Ministry and SSDF, too?" "They owe me a few favors." "lt takes one old fox to know another, l guess." Japanese Proverbs wrote: an old poacher makes the best keeper (lit.: snakes follow the path of large serpents)

[]:             Quote Post by Gendo'sPapa » Mon Feb 23, 2009 5:31 am My subs say a "Snakes always follow their path" anyone have any sub files that are just perfect for the film? Mine are very good but they have a few grammatical errors here and there (can't remember where I got it though)

[]:             Quote Post by cat42 » Mon Feb 23, 2009 9:36 am Just be patient, the FUNimation release is out in November.

[]:             Quote Post by AchtungAffen » Tue Mar 10, 2009 10:13 pm I put it as the "snake path is serpentuous (sp?)"
